search_terms_used: traditional periodization vs block periodization endurance athletes pubmed
annual training plan endurance athletes periodization pubmed
block periodization endurance cyclists VO2max pubmed
coverage_notes: Strong coverage: 1 meta-analysis (Mølmen 2019, only endurance-specific MA on block vs traditional), 3 RCTs (Stöggl 2014, Filipas 2022, Almquist 2022), 2 foundational cohort comparisons (Rønnestad 2014a/b), 2 running-specific systematic reviews (Kenneally 2018, Casado 2022), 2 theoretical reviews (Issurin 2008, 2010). Running-specific: 3 papers. Block vs traditional direct comparisons: 5 papers.

papers.title: New horizons for the methodology and physiology of training periodization
papers.notes: Reviewed limitations of traditional periodization and proposed block periodization as an alternative framework using specialized mesocycle-blocks with concentrated training workloads for high-performance athletes.

papers.title: Block periodization of endurance training - a systematic review and meta-analysis
papers.journal: Open Access Journal of Sports Medicine
papers.notes: First meta-analysis of block vs traditional periodization in endurance training, screening 2,905 studies and pooling 6 for meta-analysis of VO2max and Wmax outcomes.

papers.title: Polarized training has greater impact on key endurance variables than threshold, high intensity, or high volume training
papers.notes: Randomized 48 well-trained endurance athletes to four 9-week training intensity distribution protocols (HVT, threshold, HIIT, polarized) and compared VO2peak, time to exhaustion, and performance outcomes.

papers.title: Effects of 16 weeks of pyramidal and polarized training intensity distributions in well-trained endurance runners
papers.journal: Scandinavian Journal of Medicine and Science in Sports
papers.notes: Randomized 60 well-trained male runners to four 16-week periodization arms (pyramidal, polarized, pyramidal-to-polarized, polarized-to-pyramidal) and measured VO2peak, lactate thresholds, and 5-km time trial performance.

papers.title: Block periodization of high-intensity aerobic intervals provides superior training effects in trained cyclists
papers.journal: Scandinavian Journal of Medicine and Science in Sports
papers.notes: Compared 4-week block periodization (concentrated HIT sessions) vs traditional organization (evenly distributed HIT) in trained cyclists, measuring VO2max, Wmax, and power at 2 mmol/L lactate.

papers.title: Effects of 12 weeks of block periodization on performance and performance indices in well-trained cyclists
papers.journal: Scandinavian Journal of Medicine and Science in Sports
papers.notes: Compared 12 weeks of block periodization (5 HIT sessions every 4th week) vs traditional training (2 weekly HIT sessions) in 15 well-trained cyclists.

papers.title: No Differences Between 12 Weeks of Block- vs. Traditional-Periodized Training in Performance Adaptations in Trained Cyclists
papers.notes: Randomized 30 trained cyclists to 12 weeks of block vs traditional periodization and found equivalent time-trial performance improvements but differing hematological and capillary adaptations.

papers.title: The Effect of Periodization and Training Intensity Distribution on Middle- and Long-Distance Running Performance: A Systematic Review
papers.journal: International Journal of Sports Physiology and Performance
papers.notes: Systematically reviewed 16 studies on training intensity distribution approaches (pyramidal, polarized, threshold) specifically in middle- and long-distance runners.

papers.title: Training Periodization, Methods, Intensity Distribution, and Volume in Highly Trained and Elite Distance Runners: A Systematic Review
papers.journal: International Journal of Sports Physiology and Performance
papers.notes: Systematically reviewed periodization patterns, training methods, intensity distribution, and volume characteristics used by highly trained and elite middle- and long-distance runners.

papers.title: Block periodization versus traditional training theory: a review
papers.journal: The Journal of Sports Medicine and Physical Fitness
papers.notes: Foundational review of major contradictions between traditional periodization theory and high-performance sport demands, introducing rationale for block periodization. Pre-DOI era Minerva Medica publication.
